article_title: IEA: Global Energy Investment Hits $3.4 Trillion in 2026 as Solar Draws $365 Billion and Clean Energy Outspends Fossil Fuels Nearly 2-to-1
summary: Rigzone source returned AWS WAF bot-challenge page; Birol direct quote attributed to Rigzone is unverifiable verbatim — substance confirmed in SolarQuarter and GreentechLead but exact wording cannot be cross-checked

content_preview.title: IEA: Global Energy Investment Hits $3.4 Trillion in 2026 as Solar Draws $365 Billion and Clean Energy Outspends Fossil Fuels Nearly 2-to-1
content_preview.summary: The IEA's World Energy Investment 2026 report finds $2.2 trillion flowing to clean energy against $1.2 trillion for fossil fuels, with solar alone attracting nearly $365 billion — close to $1 billion per day.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Global energy investment is on course to reach approximately **$3.4 trillion in 2026**, a 5 percent increase from the previous year, according to the International Energy Agency's *World Energy Investment 2026* report released on May 28. The landmark annual survey finds that clean energy now commands nearly twice the capital flowing to fossil fuels — roughly **$2.2 trillion** directed toward renewables, nuclear power, electricity grids, battery storage, low-emissions fuels, efficien...

recommendations: Consider adding solarquarter.com, greentechlead.com, and rigzone.com to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive 910-word News article on the IEA World Energy Investment 2026 report. Well-structured with sections covering solar dominance, electricity infrastructure, fossil fuel trends, and the $260B savings dividend. Appropriate 'What We Don't Know' section. Good editorial balance between clean energy progress and coal/gas countertrends.
editor_notes.source_verification: Three of four sources verified from disk snapshots. source-0.html.gz (SolarQuarter): confirmed $3.4T total, $2.2T clean/$1.2T fossil split, $365B solar, $330B gas (highest in decade), $500B oil decline (third consecutive year), Birol 'largest energy security crisis in history' (paraphrase, not verbatim). source-1.html.gz (GreentechLead climate): confirmed $3.4T, $2.2T clean, $1.2T fossil, $365B solar, $200B wind, $75B hydro, 70% of power generation spending, 80% solar capex decline, $80B nuclear/78 GW/15 countries, $260B avoided import costs, China $110B savings. source-2.html.gz (GreentechLead renewables): confirmed $3.4T, $2.2T/$1.2T split, $365B solar, $330B gas, 130 GW gas plant orders/25-year high, $78 GW nuclear under construction, $260B/$110B savings, 30+ energy facilities damaged, ~20 tankers hit, $180B coal/2012 high, third consecutive oil decline below $500B. source-3.html.gz (Rigzone): returned AWS WAF bot challenge (1979 bytes). The Birol quote 'We are in the midst of the largest energy security crisis the world has ever faced' is attributed to Rigzone but cannot be verified verbatim. Substance is confirmed in SolarQuarter ('largest energy security crisis in history') and GreentechLead.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All numerical claims verified in at least one readable source. The Birol direct quote from Rigzone cannot be verified verbatim due to bot-blocking. The article presents it in direct quotes attributed to Rigzone — this is a minor concern since the bot-blocked source may have had the exact wording. SolarQuarter presents a paraphrase consistent with the quote's substance. Corrections record filed for the unverifiable quote.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and well-balanced. The article appropriately covers coal and gas countertrends alongside clean energy investment, avoiding oversimplification.
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate IEA energy investment coverage found in the archive.
concerns: Rigzone source (Birol direct quote) returned AWS WAF bot challenge — verbatim quote cannot be confirmed from snapshot
recommendations: Consider adding solarquarter.com, greentechlead.com, and rigzone.com to config/source_allowlist.txt
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong, well-sourced energy report article. Most claims fully verified. Minor concern about unverifiable Birol direct quote from bot-blocked Rigzone source — substance confirmed in other sources. Corrections record filed.
